methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $88M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Lohum Cleantech is a Series C climate and energy company founded by Rajat Verma that has raised $87.7M to date. The valuation analysis relies on two methodologies applied against a comparable universe of 4 same-stage private peers (KoBold Metals, Watershed, Electric Hydrogen, Amogy) and 1 public comparable (LNZA).

The stage-progression method applies a 1.7x multiplier to the $88M total raised proxy, implying a valuation of $149M. This approach assumes typical Series C advancement patterns but notably underweights the company relative to observable peer benchmarks. The peer-median method identifies a $1.4B median across the 4-company Climate & Energy comparator set, with individual valuations ranging from $700M (Amogy) to $2.0B (KoBold Metals). The peer-median result reflects actual market pricing for functionally similar stage and sector positioning.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $127M (low) to $1.6B (high), with a median of $775M. The substantial gap between stage-progression and peer-median outputs reflects tension between financial metrics (capital deployed) and market-based valuation. The peer-median approach is weighted more heavily given direct comparability, but material uncertainty persists around Lohum's relative technology maturity, commercial traction, and capital efficiency versus named peers. The analysis supports a midpoint estimate near $775M, with confidence bounded by the $700M–$1.4B range where comparable Series C climate companies currently trade.
